rationale: Ground truth is thin (mostly a blurred-image aside and an LGTM approval, plus one inline nit about test coverage of reduce_scatter that neither candidate addresses), so recall is low for both. X gives three concrete, well-anchored docstring/comment nits that are actionable but low-value, and one claim (enable_expert_parallel param) isn't verifiable from the shown diff. Y does deeper correctness verification (DPMetadata construction, group separation, destroy/cleanup safety, the dropped cfg_parallel_size>1 guard) that is all grounded in the diff/vLLM API and reaches an APPROVE verdict consistent with the actual LGTM outcome, though it yields only one concrete actionable comment versus X's three.
